seoTitle: How to Repeat Checklists in Trello
metaDescription: Copy an existing Trello checklist into a new card instantly using the built-in Copy Items From tool, instead of retyping subtasks.
intro: Retyping the same quality control steps or recurring process items on every new card is a waste of time when Trello can copy them for you. This guide covers pulling a checklist from an existing card into a new one in just a couple of clicks.

steps.heading: Open your target card
steps.body: Click the card where you want to add a repeated checklist.

steps.heading: Open the checklist option
steps.body: Find and click the add-checklist option on the card back.

steps.heading: Search for the source checklist
steps.body: Use the 'Copy Items From' search tool to locate the historical checklist you want to reuse.

steps.heading: Import the checklist
steps.body: Select the source checklist to bulk-import the subtask rows without losing checkbox functionality.

steps.heading: Review the imported items
steps.body: Check for any empty items or broken references and fix them if needed.

steps.heading: Keep a master template board
steps.body: Store your standard operational checklists on a dedicated template board to speed up future reuse.

faq.question: Do I lose the checkbox formatting when I copy a checklist?
faq.answer: No, the import preserves checkbox functionality and text layout from the original checklist.

faq.question: Can I copy a checklist from any card on my board?
faq.answer: Yes, the 'Copy Items From' search tool lets you locate and pull checklists from any existing card.

faq.question: What if the import doesn't work correctly?
faq.answer: Check for empty items or broken target references, which are the most common causes of replication issues.

faq.question: Where should I keep my standard checklists?
faq.answer: Storing them on a dedicated template board makes it faster to find and reuse them across future cards.
